name: High Nodal Junctional Rhythm
identifyBy: Retrograde P wave BEFORE the QRS (short PR <120ms)
P wave inverted in II, III, aVF (retrograde atrial activation)
Rate 40–60 bpm (junctional escape) or 60–100 bpm (accelerated junctional)
Impulse originates high in AV node - activates atria slightly before ventricles

measurements.pWaves: Inverted in II, III, aVF - precede QRS

management: As for junctional rhythm - treat underlying cause
Assess for AV node disease, inferior MI, digoxin toxicity
pearls: High nodal: P before QRS with short PR <120ms
Mid nodal: P hidden within QRS
Low nodal: retrograde P after QRS (RP <PR)
Differentiating nodal origin matters academically but management is the same in the acute setting
differentials: Sinus rhythm with short PR (WPW or LGL)

qaNotes: Supraventricular rhythm migrated from v1 components; verify P-wave relationship in editor.
